title: SpectraVis: Visualising and Exploring Spectra Data — SpectraVis
code: library(MsDataHub) f <- MS3TMT10_01022016_32917.33481.mzML.gz() #> see ?MsDataHub and browseVignettes('MsDataHub') for documentation #> loading from cache sp <- Spectra(f) sp #> MSn data (Spectra) with 565 spectra in a MsBackendMzR backend: #>       msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>     <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1           1   4422.62         1 #> 2           2   4422.65         2 #> 3           2   4422.67         3 #> 4           2   4422.74         4 #> 5           2   4422.80         5 #> ...       ...       ...       ... #> 561         3   4493.77       561 #> 562         3   4493.96       562 #> 563         2   4493.98       563 #> 564         3   4494.14       564 #> 565         3   4494.34       565 #>  ... 34 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 31c07e7afc179a_10477  # if (interactive()) #   browseSpectra(sp)  ## Use Ctrl+C to interrupt R and stop the application  # if (interactive()) #   plotlySpectra(sp[1])

what: Browse spectra in a Spectra object — browseSpectra
title: Browse spectra in a Spectra object — browseSpectra
text: browseSpectra() function opens simple shiny application allows browse trough individual scans Spectra object. title scans contains MS level, retention time, available precursor m/z precursor charge peptides sequence (defined sequence spectra variable) index scan. scans sequence (defined sequence spectra variable), matching fragments (defined PSMatch::calculateFragments() labelled, otherwise intense peaks (defined intensity high half highest peak) labelled m/z. See ?SpectraVis example.

what: Interactive visualisation of a single spectrum — plotlySpectra
title: Interactive visualisation of a single spectrum — plotlySpectra
text: plotlySpectra() function displays single spectrum stored Spectra object using plotly::plot_ly() function. See ?SpectraVis example.

what: Interactive visualisation of a mirror plot — plotlySpectraMirror
title: Interactive visualisation of a mirror plot — plotlySpectraMirror
text: plotlySpectraMirror function creates interactive mirror plot comparing two spectra x y . Peaks matching m/z values (considering ppm tolerance) indicated point size can specified parameter matchSize. See also plotSpectraMirror() non-interactive version plot.

title: Interactive visualisation of a mirror plot — plotlySpectraMirror
code: plotlySpectraMirror(   x,   y,   xLabel = "",   xColor = "#737373",   yLabel = "",   yColor = "#737373",   matchSize = 5,   ppm = 20,   tolerance = 0 )

title: Interactive visualisation of a mirror plot — plotlySpectraMirror
text: x Spectra() object length 1. y Spectra() object length 1. xLabel Optional character(1) label (name) x. xColor Color peaks spectrum x. yLabel Optional character(1) label (name) y. yColor Color peaks spectrum y. matchSize numeric(1) defining size point used indicate peaks x y matching m/z. ppm numeric(1) m/z relative acceptable difference (ppm) peaks considered matching (see common() details). tolerance numeric(1) absolute acceptable difference m/z values peaks considered matching (see common() details).
